---
title: Student & Instructor versions
---

Some teaching materials keep exercises and answers in one source document,
then render a learner-facing output and an answer-bearing output. This avoids
maintaining two copies of the same exercise text.

## Names vary by repository

Existing projects use different labels for the same broad distinction:

| Learner-facing label | Answer-bearing label | Example use |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `exercise` | `solution` | Website pages containing hidden answer callouts |
| `student` | `instructor` | Report or notebook outputs |
| `student` | `master` | Website, notebook, and PDF builds |

Use the labels declared in the repository's task and profile files.

## Content-selection patterns

One website project marks an answer block with a custom solution callout:

```markdown
::: {.callout-solution}
Answer text or worked solution.
:::
```

Notebook-oriented projects may tag executable cells:

<pre><code>```&#123;python&#125;
#| solution: true
print("Answer-bearing calculation")
```

```&#123;python&#125;
#| exercise: true
#| eval: false
... # Learner starting point
```</code></pre>

These syntaxes function only when the project's filters, extensions, and
profiles support them. They are examples of existing patterns, not a
universal authoring contract.

## Check both views

When editing material affected by visibility rules:

1. Preview or render the learner-facing output and confirm answers are absent
   while instructions and starter material remain usable.
2. Preview or render the answer-bearing output and confirm worked solutions,
   code, and any instructor-specific guidance are visible.
3. Keep shared statements outside filtered blocks unless the text genuinely
   differs between audiences.
